heading: Mandatory detention of suspected terrorists; habeas corpus; judicial review
label: 1226a Mandatory detention of suspected terrorists; habeas corpus; judicial review

L. 107–56, title IV, § 412(c)115 Stat. 352Oct. 26, 2001“Not later than 6 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], and every 6 months thereafter, the Attorney General shall submit a report to the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate, with respect to the reporting period, on—Oct. 26, 2001“(1)the number of aliens certified under section 236A(a)(3) of the Immigration and Nationality Act [], as added by subsection (a);8 U.S.C. 1226a(a)(3)“(2)the grounds for such certifications;“(3)the nationalities of the aliens so certified;“(4)the length of the detention for each alien so certified; and“(5)the number of aliens so certified who—“(A)were granted any form of relief from removal;“(B)were removed;“(C)the Attorney General has determined are no longer aliens who may be so certified; or“(D)were released from detention.”

children.heading: Custody
children.label: 1 Custody
children.text: The Attorney General shall take into custody any alien who is certified under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Release
children.label: 2 Release
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (5) and (6), the Attorney General shall maintain custody of such an alien until the alien is removed from the United States. Except as provided in paragraph (6), such custody shall be maintained irrespective of any relief from removal for which the alien may be eligible, or any relief from removal granted the alien, until the Attorney General determines that the alien is no longer an alien who may be certified under paragraph (3). If the alien is finally determined not to be removable, detention pursuant to this subsection shall terminate.

children.label: A
children.text: is described in section 1182(a)(3)(A)(i), 1182(a)(3)(A)(iii), 1182(a)(3)(B), 1227(a)(4)(A)(i), 1227(a)(4)(A)(iii), or 1227(a)(4)(B) of this title; or

children.label: B
children.text: is engaged in any other activity that endangers the national security of the United States.

children.heading: Nondelegation
children.label: 4 Nondelegation
children.text: The Attorney General may delegate the authority provided under paragraph (3) only to the Deputy Attorney General. The Deputy Attorney General may not delegate such authority.

children.heading: Commencement of proceedings
children.label: 5 Commencement of proceedings
children.text: The Attorney General shall place an alien detained under paragraph (1) in removal proceedings, or shall charge the alien with a criminal offense, not later than 7 days after the commencement of such detention. If the requirement of the preceding sentence is not satisfied, the Attorney General shall release the alien.

children.heading: Limitation on indefinite detention
children.label: 6 Limitation on indefinite detention
children.text: An alien detained solely under paragraph (1) who has not been removed under , and whose removal is unlikely in the reasonably foreseeable future, may be detained for additional periods of up to six months only if the release of the alien will threaten the national security of the United States or the safety of the community or any person.section 1231(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Review of certification
children.label: 7 Review of certification
children.text: The Attorney General shall review the certification made under paragraph (3) every 6 months. If the Attorney General determines, in the Attorney General’s discretion, that the certification should be revoked, the alien may be released on such conditions as the Attorney General deems appropriate, unless such release is otherwise prohibited by law. The alien may request each 6 months in writing that the Attorney General reconsider the certification and may submit documents or other evidence in support of that request.

children.heading: Habeas corpus and judicial review
children.label: b Habeas corpus and judicial review

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Judicial review of any action or decision relating to this section (including judicial review of the merits of a determination made under subsection (a)(3) or (a)(6)) is available exclusively in habeas corpus proceedings consistent with this subsection. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, no court shall have jurisdiction to review, by habeas corpus petition or otherwise, any such action or decision.

children.label: ii
children.text: any justice of the Supreme Court;

children.label: iii
children.text: any circuit judge of the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit; or

children.label: iv
children.text: any district court otherwise having jurisdiction to entertain it.

children.heading: Application transfer
children.label: B Application transfer
children.text: shall apply to an application for a writ of habeas corpus described in subparagraph (A).Section 2241(b) of title 28

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: 3 Appeals
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, including , in habeas corpus proceedings described in paragraph (1) before a circuit or district judge, the final order shall be subject to review, on appeal, by the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit. There shall be no right of appeal in such proceedings to any other circuit court of appeals.section 2253 of title 28

children.heading: Rule of decision
children.label: 4 Rule of decision
children.text: The law applied by the Supreme Court and the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit shall be regarded as the rule of decision in habeas corpus proceedings described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Statutory construction
children.label: c Statutory construction
children.text: The provisions of this section shall not be applicable to any other provision of this chapter.
